title: Manual time-keeping entry - allow to 'end in the future'
excerpt:
It would be great if when entering time manually, we could ‘round up’ a bit to the next nearest ~15 minute interval (perhaps make that a setting the administrators can control). 
Our organization doesn’t care about time …

posts.author: AndrewCI (Andrew Albosta)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be great if when entering time manually, we could ‘round up’ a bit to the next nearest ~15 minute interval (perhaps make that a setting the administrators can control).
Our organization doesn’t care about time tracking to the minute, and if I’m trying to log ‘9a - 9:45a’ but the time is currently 9:42a, the system will throw an error that ‘the end time can’t be in the future’

I’d like to be able to override that error instead of needing to remember to come back and fill out time tracking in a few minutes, or having to enter ‘8:55a-9:40a’.

posts.author: MistyMK (AppBay)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @AndrewCI there is a new app that allows you to just amend the duration rather then fiddle with start and stop times. The report will show original data and amended finalised duration.

The app is called Smart Timer and Team Tracking, same look and feel as the native app but can just do more. You can have two people simultaneously record time, see live what people are recording on and view a detailed reporting board (and export the report).

Hope that helps.
